functions: Tumor suppressor
Transcription factor regulating cell cycle arrest and apoptosis
Mediator of DNA damage response
Regulator of cardiomyocyte apoptosis and oxidative stress response (p53)
pubmed.druggability_notes: TP53 pathway is targeted indirectly via MDM2-p53 antagonists. The MDM2-p53 antagonist brigimadlin (Brightline-1 trial, NCT05218499) activated the TP53 pathway in MDM2-amplified dedifferentiated liposarcoma, though no predictive efficacy biomarkers were identified. Quercetin and luteolin showed moderate predicted binding affinity to p53 in molecular docking studies related to AKT1/p53 signaling in heart failure.

functions: Sequence-specific DNA-binding transcription factor that activates or represses target genes via RNA polymerase II
Induces cell cycle arrest, including via transcription of cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitors
Induces apoptosis by stimulating BAX and FAS expression and repressing BCL2
Promotes DNA repair (including nucleotide-excision repair) upon DNA damage
Acts as a tumor suppressor inducing growth arrest, senescence or apoptosis
Activates oxidative stress-induced necrosis in cooperation with mitochondrial PPIF, largely transcription-independent
Prevents CDK7 kinase activity within the CAK complex to halt cell cycle progression after DNA damage
Regulates the circadian clock by repressing CLOCK-BMAL1-mediated transcriptional activation of PER2
pathways: TP53 Regulates Transcription of Cell Cycle Genes
TP53 Regulates Transcription of Genes Involved in Apoptosis
DNA Damage Response (signal transduction by p53 class mediator)
uniprot.druggability_notes: Zinc/metal-binding transcription factor with sequence-specific DNA-binding domain; activity is regulated by interaction with the MDM2/MDM4 family of E3 ligases (MDM2/MDM4 binding noted), a well-known druggable axis for p53 reactivation. Pro-apoptotic activity is modulated by ASPP-family proteins (PPP1R13B/ASPP1, TP53BP2/ASPP2 activating; PPP1R13L/iASPP inhibiting), representing protein-protein interaction interfaces. 3D-structure available.

functions: Sequence-specific DNA-binding transcription factor that activates target genes to induce cell cycle arrest, DNA repair, or apoptosis
Tumor suppressor inducing growth arrest or apoptosis depending on cellular context
Negatively regulates cell division by controlling expression of genes including a cyclin-dependent kinase inhibitor (p21/CDKN1A)
Induces apoptosis via stimulation of BAX and FAS expression or repression of Bcl-2
Cooperates with mitochondrial PPIF to activate oxidative stress-induced necrosis independent of transcription
Induces transcription of lincRNA-p21 mediating TP53-dependent transcriptional repression and apoptosis
Prevents CDK7 kinase activity within the CAK complex in response to DNA damage to halt cell cycle progression
Regulates the circadian clock by repressing CLOCK-BMAL1-mediated transcription of PER2
pathways: Regulation of TP53 Activity through Phosphorylation (R-HSA-6804756)

TP53 Regulates Transcription of Genes Involved in Cytochrome C Release (R-HSA-6803204)
Activation of PUMA and translocation to mitochondria (R-HSA-139915)
Activation of NOXA and translocation to mitochondria (R-HSA-111448)
Transcriptional activation of cell cycle inhibitor p21 (R-HSA-69895)
Loss of function of TP53 in cancer due to loss of tetramerization ability (R-HSA-9723905)
cellular_states: cells undergoing G1/G2 cell cycle arrest

disease_associations.disease: Head and neck squamous cell carcinoma

opentargets.druggability_notes: The text notes loss of function of TP53 in cancer due to loss of tetramerization ability, implicating the tetramerization domain as a potential structural target. Pro-apoptotic activity is modulated by ASPP-family interactions (PPP1R13B/ASPP1, TP53BP2/ASPP2 activating; PPP1R13L/iASPP inhibiting), suggesting protein-protein interaction interfaces of pharmacological interest. No explicit small-molecule binding pocket or drug class is described in the provided text.
